translation.en: I do not like to argue with you.

exampleSentences: Anh và em không nên cãi nhau về chuyện nhỏ như vậy.
Mỗi lần bị mẹ mắng, em thường cãi lại để giải thích ý mình.
Bạn bè thân thiết cũng có thể cãi vì hiểu lầm trong cuộc sống.
notes:
cãi = to argue or quarrel with someone, usually in a heated or emotional way. It’s used when people disagree and talk back and forth.

It often goes with nhau when two sides argue: cãi nhau (argue with each other). You can also say cãi + ai (argue with someone) or cãi lại (talk back / argue back, often to someone older or in authority).

Close synonym: tranh cãi (more formal, like “debate” or “dispute”). Opposite vibe: hòa thuận (get along peacefully).
notes_md:
**cãi** = to argue or quarrel with someone, usually in a heated or emotional way. It’s used when people disagree and talk back and forth.

It often goes with **nhau** when two sides argue: *cãi nhau* (argue with each other). You can also say **cãi + ai** (argue with someone) or **cãi lại** (talk back / argue back, often to someone older or in authority).


Close synonym: **tranh cãi** (more formal, like “debate” or “dispute”). Opposite vibe: **hòa thuận** (get along peacefully).
exampleSentencesTranslated: You and I should not argue about such small things.
Every time mom scolds me, I often argue back to explain my point.
Close friends can also argue because of misunderstandings in life.

definitions.en: To argue or dispute; to disagree verbally.
definitions.es: Discutir o disputar; no estar de acuerdo verbalmente.
definitions.vi: Cãi nhau hoặc tranh luận; không đồng ý bằng lời nói.

example.vi: Họ thường cãi nhau về những vấn đề nhỏ.
example.es: A menudo discuten sobre problemas pequeños.
example.en: They often argue about small issues.
